---
title: "The Female Reproductive System"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    center: true
---

## Overview

- The female reproductive system is responsible for reproduction, hormone production, and menstruation
- It includes both external and internal structures
- It works in coordination with the endocrine system

---

## Main Functions

- Production of eggs (ova)
- Fertilization and support of embryo development
- Menstrual cycle regulation
- Hormone production (estrogen and progesterone)

---

## External Structures (Vulva)

- **Labia majora**: outer protective folds
- **Labia minora**: inner folds protecting openings
- **Clitoris**: highly sensitive erectile tissue
- **Vaginal opening**: entrance to internal reproductive tract

---

## Internal Structures

- **Vagina**: muscular canal connecting cervix to outside body
- **Cervix**: lower part of uterus that opens into vagina
- **Uterus**: muscular organ where fetal development occurs
- **Fallopian tubes**: transport eggs to uterus
- **Ovaries**: produce eggs and hormones

---

## The Menstrual Cycle

- Average cycle: ~28 days (varies widely)
- Four phases:
  - Menstrual phase
  - Follicular phase
  - Ovulation
  - Luteal phase

---

## Ovulation

- Occurs mid-cycle
- Release of a mature egg from the ovary
- Egg travels into the fallopian tube
- Peak fertility period

---

## Fertilization

- Happens in the fallopian tube
- Sperm meets egg → zygote formation
- Zygote travels to uterus for implantation

---

## Pregnancy and Uterus

- Uterus lining thickens to support implantation
- Placenta forms to nourish developing embryo
- Pregnancy lasts about 40 weeks on average

---

## Hormonal Control

- **Estrogen**: development of female traits, regulates cycle
- **Progesterone**: prepares uterus for pregnancy
- Controlled by hypothalamus and pituitary gland

---

## Common Health Conditions

- Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
- Endometriosis
- Uterine fibroids
- Ovarian cysts

---

## Importance of Reproductive Health

- Regular medical checkups are essential
- Awareness of menstrual cycle changes
- Early detection of disorders improves outcomes
- Reproductive health affects overall well-being

---

## Summary

- The system supports reproduction and hormonal balance
- Includes external and internal organs working together
- Regulated by complex hormonal cycles

---

## Questions?

- Thank you for your attention!
